metadata.title: GCC Integrated Customs Tariff Data - Research Findings
metadata.scope: GCC member states customs tariff information
metadata.research_status: Completed - Web search and portal validation

executive_summary.overview: In December 2024, the GCC Customs Union Authority issued the first edition of the GCC Integrated Customs Tariff to be implemented from January 1, 2025, by all GCC member states. The system expanded from 8-digit to 12-digit tariff codes, increasing tariff lines from 7,800 to over 13,400 lines.

tariff_code_structure.description: The 12-digit GCC Integrated Customs Tariff structure
tariff_code_structure.basis: Based on the 2022 edition of the Harmonized System for Description and Classification (HS) developed by the World Customs Organization

bahrain.features: HS Code search and tariff finder available
bahrain.notes: Updates may have been pending as of February 2025

kuwait.standard_duty_rate: 5% on CIF value for most goods
kuwait.preferential_rate: 0% for food products, medicines, fresh plants/animals, gold, essential consumer goods, and printing materials
kuwait.notes: Kuwait is one of the three member states that have officially published the 12-digit tariff schedule

oman.special_notes: Digital tax stamps on imported beverages effective 2025-02-01
oman.notes: Oman was among the first to publish the 12-digit tariff schedule

qatar.notes: Qatar was among the first to publish the 12-digit tariff schedule

saudi_arabia.tariff_access: ZATCA - Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority
saudi_arabia.notes: Saudi Arabia previously used a 12-digit system, now transitioning to GCC Integrated format

uae_emirates.tariff_access_federal: ICP - Federal Authority for Identity, Citizenship, Customs & Ports Security (icp.gov.ae)
uae_emirates.notes: Dubai initially implemented on Jan 1, 2025; full UAE implementation on Aug 1, 2025

dubai_customs.documents: Integrated Customs Tariff v4.0 PDF available

wto_resources.description: World Customs Organization HS Code database
wto_resources.harmonized_system: HS 2022 Edition used as basis for GCC Integrated Tariff

successful_implementations: Qatar has officially published and implemented the 12-digit tariff schedule as of January 1, 2025
Kuwait has officially published and implemented the 12-digit tariff schedule as of January 1, 2025
Oman has officially published and implemented the 12-digit tariff schedule as of January 1, 2025
Saudi Arabia transitioned to the GCC Integrated format on January 1, 2025
pending_implementations: Bahrain implementation announced; full rollout status pending as of February 2025
UAE phased implementation - Dubai on January 1, 2025; full UAE on August 1, 2025
data_access_methods: Official customs authority portals with HS code search functionality
Tariff finder/search tools on each country's customs website
PDF documents and user manuals available for download
Online inquiry systems for duty rates and classifications
challenges_encountered: Direct Excel/bulk download files not readily available through public search
Most systems require manual searching through web interfaces
Some PDFs are binary-encoded and require specialized extraction
Implementation timelines vary by member state

data_collection_strategy: Access each country's customs authority portal directly for the most current tariff data
Use the HS code search interfaces for lookups and bulk data collection
Contact customs authorities directly for bulk export options (Excel/CSV)
Check for API access or data export features on official portals
next_steps: Verify current availability of bulk Excel exports from each customs authority
Document any API endpoints or data export capabilities
Create standardized mapping between member state codes and international HS codes
Track any additional implementation notices or amendments from GCC Customs Union Authority
